name: Instructor Example
headline: Apple picks Google's Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri — paying ~$1 billion per year
hook:
Apple already pays Google roughly $20 billion a year to be the default search engine on every iPhone.
  This deal adds another billion for AI. The most profitable hardware company in history has now made Google the
  invisible backbone of its two most important user interfaces...
analysis:
Five Forces — Supplier Power: Apple and Google have long had a mutually dependent relationship.
  Google needed iPhone distribution; Apple needed search revenue. The bargaining looked roughly balanced. This deal
  changes that...
question:
Apple's differentiation has historically required controlling every layer of the experience. If the
  intelligence layer is now Google's, what exactly is Apple differentiating on — and is that enough to justify the
  premium over Android?
